title: audit-envelope v1.0.0
description: Hash-chained audit record envelope. Every call to interlock's AuditChain.append() emits exactly one record matching this schema. The envelope is exhaustive: any field a consumer needs to act on must be named here. Event names are governed by governance/audit-event-ownership.md (not enum-locked in this schema so adding events does not churn the contract). See README.md for field semantics, hash-algorithm pin, and chain-genesis convention.

seq.description: Monotonic sequence number within the chain. The chain_genesis record is seq=0; each subsequent record increments by one.

prev_hash.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the previous record_hash. The chain_genesis record uses 64 zero hex characters (see README §Chain genesis).

record_hash.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of this record's canonical serialization (see README §Canonical serialization).

wall_time.description: Wall-clock timestamp (epoch seconds, UTC) at which AuditChain.append() ran. Not monotonic across reboots.

monotonic_ns.description: Process-local monotonic clock at append time, in nanoseconds (Python time.monotonic_ns()). Only ordered within a single process lifetime.

event.description: Event name. Namespace governed by governance/audit-event-ownership.md; this schema does not enum-lock it so adding events is a governance change, not a contract bump.

emitter.description: Layer name that called AuditChain.append(). Required so audit-verify can enforce the cross-layer ownership rule (e.g., gate_frozen must have emitter='sieve').

labels.description: Event-specific structured payload. Per-event shape documented alongside the event in governance/audit-event-ownership.md. Open object: keys and value types are event-defined.

categories.description: Sieve classifier categories for events that carry a verdict; null otherwise.

confidence.description: Sieve classifier confidence in [0, 1] for events that carry a verdict; null otherwise.

pattern_hash.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the pattern_table HashInput active when the event was emitted (see integrity-inputs/v1.0.0); null when not applicable.

model_digest.description: Raw model manifest digest of the model_digest HashInput active when the event was emitted (see integrity-inputs/v1.0.0). Format is whatever the Ollama manifest endpoint returns; not necessarily hex SHA-256. Null when not applicable.

prompt_hash.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the prompt_template HashInput active when the event was emitted (see integrity-inputs/v1.0.0); null when not applicable.
